rulings.Case Title: SF URBAN FOREST COALITION, A CALIFORNIA VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A CHARTER CITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney Fees
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, August 22, 2018, Line 1, PETITIONER SF URBAN FOREST COALITION's Motion For Attorney Fees Petitioner SF Urban Forest Coalition's motion for attorney fees is granted in part. SFUFC is awarded fees against respondent San Francisco County Transportation Authority in the amount of $75,000, calculated as 40 hours of Mr. Grossman's time at the hourly rate of $600, 90 hours of Mr. Seavy's time at the hourly rate of Mr. Seavy's time of $400, and 60 hours of Mr. Chaffee's time at the hourly rate of $150. The requested hourly rates for Mr. Grossman and Mr. Chaffee are reasonable and within the range for an attorney and a paralegal with their experience and skills. The $550 requested hourly rate for Mr. Seavy, who has been practicing law for only four years and appears to be a solo practitioner, is far in excess of what is within the reasonable range for an attorney with his skill and experience. $400 per hour is at the high end of the range for an attorney with Mr. Seavy's skill and experience. The recoverable hours claimed by SFUFC far exceeds what is reasonable for a fairly pedestrian California Public Records Act case, and taking into account that SFUFC did not prevail on the only arguably difficult and novel issue (the applicability of the Sunshine Ordinance) that was litigated. While the court does not doubt that the three timekeepers spent all the time they say they spent on this case, spending the time does not make the spent time recoverable. Excluding all time spent on the Sunshine Ordinance issues and all time spent after April 25, 2018 except for time on this motion and significantly reducing the hours spent on the core CPRA claim due to the unreasonableness of those hours and the fact that they included work on matters that went well beyond the relatively small number of documents sought by SFUFC on its pleaded CPRA claim, the court finds that the high end of recoverable time is 40 hours by Mr. Grossman, 90 hours by Mr. Seavy, and 60 hours by Mr. Chaffee. The significant reduction in the amount awarded from the amount sought is not intended, expressly or impliedly, as a criticism of the work of Mr. Grossman, Mr. Seavy and/or Mr. Chaffee, but merely that the great majority of the hours claimed by them and the hourly rate sought for the services of Mr. Seavy are far out of line for the success they achieved on SFUFC's CPRA claim. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for SFCTA is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=302/hk

rulings.Case Title: CACH, LLC VS. WILLIAM T TAYLOR
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Defualt Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, August 22, 2018, Line 2, DEFENDANT WILLIAM TAYLOR's Motion To Set Aside Default And Defualt Judgment Defendant William Taylor's motion to set aside the default entered against him on April 17, 2017 and the default judgment entered against him on April 22, 2018 is continued to September 6, 2018 to give Mr. Taylor an opportunity to file a proposed answer which it appears he inadvertently failed to do. Mr. Taylor has until August 28, 2018 to file a proposed answer and, if he does, plaintiff CACH, LLC has until September 4, 2018 to file any objection to that proposed answer. Assuming that Mr. Taylor files a proposed answer in proper form, the court intends to grant Mr. Taylor's motion to set aside the default and default judgment and deny the motion to quash service of summons. CACH has not submitted sufficient evidence to refute Mr. Taylor's statement under penalty of perjury that he first learned of this lawsuit on or around July 16, 2018 and that he was not evading service. Those statements are sufficient to warrant setting aside the default and default judgment. rulings.Case Title: STEPHANIE RICO VS. OLIVIA ABRAHAM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, August 22, 2018, Line 3, DEFENDANT LUIS VERA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Luis Vera's motion for summary judgment is granted. Viewing the undisputed facts in the light most favorable to plaintiff Stephanie Rico, those facts are insufficient to show that Mr. Vera had a duty to protect Ms. Vera from the assault and battery she suffered from a co-passenger in the vehicle driven by Mr. Vera. The undisputed facts do not support the existence of a special relationship between Mr. Vera and Ms. Rico for purposes of imposing a duty on Mr. Vera to protect Ms. Vera from the unanticipated violent conduct of Ms. Abraham. (California Body & Trailer Mfrs. v. Albrecht (1971) 16 Cal.App.3d Supp. 1, 3-4 ("The driver of an automobile must manage his vehicle so as not to create an unreasonable risk of harm to others, but in the absence of facts putting the driver [on notice of likely misconduct of another passenger]. . .the driver is under no duty to persons who may be injured by the negligence of the guest. Ordinarily, in the absence of a special relationship between the parties, there is no duty to control the conduct of a third person so as to prevent him from causing harm to another.") (citations omitted)). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JOHN N. IANNUCCILLO ET AL VS. O'NEILL CONSTRUCTION, INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant And Cross-Complainant Oneill Construction, Inc. For Good Faith Settlement Determination
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, august 22, 2018, line 4, DEFENDANT O'NEILL CONSTRUCTION, INC's Motion Of Defendant And Cross-Complainant Oneill Construction, Inc.'s For Good Faith Settlement Determination Defendant O'Neill Construction, Inc.'s motion for a good faith determination of its settlement with plaintiffs John and Ann Iannuccillo is granted. O'Neill has satisfied its burden to provide the information required for a good faith determination per Tech-Bilt and cross-defendant Treanor Tiling, the only party to oppose this motion, has not shown that the settlement is either outside the "ballpark" or any other reason warranting the denial of this motion. Treanor's reliance on Radford for the proposition that OCI must provide a copy of the settlement agreement to Treanor is misplaced. Unlike what occurred in Radford, Treanor has been provided the material terms of the settlement. Contrary to Treanor's assertion that it will not receive any offset per CCP 877(a), OCI has allocated portions of the settlement to Stearman costs and the Iannucillos' "out-of-pocket" costs, as well as valued its assignment of its cross-claims. The court is not aware of any authority requiring OCI to provide a valuation for each of the two assignments of cross-claims nor does there appear to be a need to do so in this case. As argued by OCI, the valuations and allocations made by a settling party for a good faith motion are not binding on the trial court if the remainder of case proceeds to trial. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for OCI is required to prepare a proposed order which includes language comporting with CCP 877 regarding the effect of granting this motion as well as repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: MARILYN M. SANTIAGO VS. DIGNITY HEALTH, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, August 22, 2018, Line 5, Part 1 of 2 tentative rulings, Defendants Dignity Health, Inc. and St. Francis Memorial Hospital's motion for summary judgment is granted. The undisputed evidence shows that defendants are entitled to judgment in their favor on all four of the causes of action alleged in the third amended complaint filed by plaintiff Marilyn Santiago. Ms. Santiago's first cause of action for failure to reasonably accommodate lacks merit because she has not identified any reasonable accommodation that would enable her to perform her job as a nurse. "The FEHA 'does not prohibit an employer from . . . discharging an employee with a physical or mental disability . . . where the employee, because of his or her physical or mental disability, is unable to perform his or her essential duties even with reasonable accommodations . . . .'" (Scotch v. Art Institute of California (2009) 173 Cal.App.4th 986, 1005 citing Government Code 12940(a)(1).). The undisputed facts show that Ms. Santiago could not perform her essential job duties with or without a reasonable accommodation at the time her employment was terminated. Lifting was a necessary part of her nursing job. Ms. Santiago citation to the portion of the Collective Bargaining Agreement stating that efforts should be made to reduce lifting is unavailing. While finite leave can be a reasonable accommodation, a "[r]easonable accommodation does not require the employer to wait indefinitely for an employee's medical condition to be corrected . . . ." (Hanson v. Lucky Stores, Inc. (1999) 74 Cal.App.4th 215, 226-27.) Ms. Santiago acknowledged that, when her employment was terminated, she had no idea when she could return to work because of the uncertainty when she would be able to do the required lifting. Her attorney's 4/28/15 e-mail, which asked for 3 months of leave, also stated that it was unclear when she could return to work. Ms. Santiago's second and third causes of action for age and disability discrimination lack merit because defendants presented a legitimate reason for the termination of Ms. Santiago's employment and Ms. Santiago did not present substantial responsive evidence of pretext. Prior to the termination of her employment, Ms. Santiago had not worked at the hospital for several months and was not on approved leave. (Continued in Part 2 of 2)

rulings.Case Title: MARILYN M. SANTIAGO VS. DIGNITY HEALTH, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication (Amended)
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, August 22, 2018, Line 5, Part 2 of 2 tentative rulings, DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH, INC., ST. FRANCISCO MEMORIAL HOSPITAL MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication (Amended) Defendants told Ms. Santiago to apply for leave and provide documentation or she would be considered absent without leave. Ms. Santiago failed to do so and her employment was terminated. While Ms. Santiago shows that she attempted to apply for leave and did not do so because of a misunderstanding or miscommunication, the law is settled that a non-discriminatory error in judgment by an employer is not discrimination. To show that an employer's proffered reason for termination of an employee's employment is pretextual, an employee "cannot simply show that the employer's decision was wrong or mistaken, since the factual dispute at issue is whether discriminatory animus motivated the employer, not whether the employer is wise, shrewd, prudent, or competent."' (Hersant v. Department of Social Services (1997) 57 Cal.App.4th 997, 1005.) Moreover, Ms. Santiago impermissibly relies on her third amended complaint in an attempt to create a triable issue regarding pretext. (Defendants' Undisputed Material Facts 38 and 75.) Ms. Santiago's fourth cause of action for breach of an implied contract claim fails because the undisputed evidence shows that the California Nurses Association represents nurses such as Ms. Santiago and the hospital and CNA entered into a CBA. The CBA governs the parties' contractual relationship and federal law preempts Ms. Santiago's common law contract claim. (Levy v. Skywalker Sound (2003) 108 Cal.App.4th 753.). rulings.Case Title: KARA L. ESBORG VS. COMCAST CORP. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records Issued July 11, 2018 To O.C. Communications
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 22, 2018, Line 2, DEFENDANT COMCAST OF CALIFORNIA III, INC.'S d Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records Issued July 11, 2018 To O.C. Communications. Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Judge Pro Tem Recuses himself from hearing this matter. Continued to September 4th. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcatalanotti@fmglaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MARCO ANTONIO RECINOS VS. USF REDDAWAY, INC. A OREGON CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder To Plaintiffs Motion For An Order Striking Defendants Nguyens Designated Experts
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 22, 2018, Line 9, DEFENDANTS USF REDDAWAY, INC. AND EMMANUEL ESTACIO'S Joinder To Plaintiff's Motion For An Order Striking Defendants Nguyens Designated Experts. USF Reddaway, Inc. and Emmanuel Estacio's joinder to plaintiff's motion for an order striking Defendants Nguyen's designated experts is granted. Plaintiff Marcos Recinos' motion to strike the Nguyen Defendants' supplemental expert witness designation is granted as to Kimberly Hawthorne and denied as to Edward Younger and Howard Friedman. As indicated by their initial expert witness designation, the Nguyen Defendants were aware that they may need or desire an expert regarding "pallet jack safety" yet did not designate an expert on that subject in their initial expert designation. Since Mr. Recinos (the only other party to serve an initial designation) did not designate an expert on that subject in his initial designation, absent evidence of a credible excuse by the Nguyen Defendants and lack of legal prejudice to Mr. Recinos, the Nguyen Defendants are precluded from designating such an expert in their supplemental disclosures. The Nguyen Defendants did not provide evidence of a credible excuse for their failure to designate a safety expert in the initial designation. On the other hand, the Nguyen Defendants did provide evidence of a credible excuse for their failure to designate Dr. Younger and Dr. Friedman in their initial designation. Their credible excuse is that, based on their counsel's discussions with counsel for defendants USF Reddaway, Inc. and Emmanuel Estacio, the Nguyen Defendants did not designate Dr. Younger and Dr. Friedman as retained experts because they reasonably believed USF and Mr. Estacio would designate them as retained experts in their initial disclosures. USF and Mr. Estacio did not designate Dr. Younger and Dr. Friedman as retained experts because of a settlement between Mr. Recinos, on the one hand, and USF and Mr. Estacio, on the other, which was designed in part to ensure that the Nguyen Defendants would be unable to designate Dr. Younger and Dr. Friedman as retained experts. The effort to ensure that the Nguyen Defendants would be unable to designate Dr. Younger and Dr. Friedman as retained experts violates fair dealing principles in litigation and is entitled to no respect. Mr. Recinos has been on notice for many months that Dr. Younger and Dr. Friedman would be experts in this case and thus Dr. Recions has not shown, nor is he able to show, that he is legally prejudiced by permitting the Nguyen Defendants to designate Dr. Younger and Dr. Friedman as their experts. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Nguyen Defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 22, 2018, Line 9, Plaintiff'S Motion To Strike The Supplemental Expert Witness Designation Filed By Defendants Lisa Nguyen Dba Bay'S Nail & Beauty Supply, Thanh Nguyen, Cung Duound And Xinh Nguyen Aka Lisa Nguyen. Plaintiff Marcos Recinos' motion to strike the Nguyen Defendants' supplemental expert witness designation is granted as to Kimberly Hawthorne and denied as to Edward Younger and Howard Friedman. As indicated by their initial expert witness designation, the Nguyen Defendants were aware that they may need or desire an expert regarding "pallet jack safety" yet did not designate an expert on that subject in their initial expert designation. Since Mr. Recinos (the only other party to serve an initial designation) did not designate an expert on that subject in his initial designation, absent evidence of a credible excuse by the Nguyen Defendants and lack of legal prejudice to Mr. Recinos, the Nguyen Defendants are precluded from designating such an expert in their supplemental disclosures. The Nguyen Defendants did not provide evidence of a credible excuse for their failure to designate a safety expert in the initial designation. On the other hand, the Nguyen Defendants did provide evidence of a credible excuse for their failure to designate Dr. Younger and Dr. Friedman in their initial designation. Their credible excuse is that, based on their counsel's discussions with counsel for defendants USF Reddaway, Inc. and Emmanuel Estacio, the Nguyen Defendants did not designate Dr. Younger and Dr. Friedman as retained experts because they reasonably believed USF and Mr. Estacio would designate them as retained experts in their initial disclosures. USF and Mr. Estacio did not designate Dr. Younger and Dr. Friedman as retained experts because of a settlement between Mr. Recinos, on the one hand, and USF and Mr. Estacio, on the other, which was designed in part to ensure that the Nguyen Defendants would be unable to designate Dr. Younger and Dr. Friedman as retained experts. The effort to ensure that the Nguyen Defendants would be unable to designate Dr. Younger and Dr. Friedman as retained experts violates fair dealing principles in litigation and is entitled to no respect. Mr. Recinos has been on notice for many months that Dr. Younger and Dr. Friedman would be experts in this case and thus Dr. Recions has not shown, nor is he able to show, that he is legally prejudiced by permitting the Nguyen Defendants to designate Dr. Younger and Dr. Friedman as their experts. rulings.Case Title: MUSIC GROUP COMMERCIAL BM LTD VS. TONY KARAVIDAS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 22, 2018, Line 6, DEFENDANT DAVE SMITH INSTRUMENTS LLC'S Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs. Defendant Dave Smith Instruments LLC's motion for attorneys' fees and costs is granted in part. DSI is awarded fees and costs against plaintiff Music Group Commercial BM Ltd. in the amount of $40,845.92, calculated as 124 hours of Mr. Shryrock's time at the billed hourly rate of $185 plus 110.6 hours of Ms. Varnica's time at the billed hourly rate of $170 less a negative .1 multiplier for all the attorney hours plus costs of $3278.12. The billed hourly rates represent the market rate for the time spent by DSI's attorneys on DSI's successful anti-SLAPP motion. There is no reason why DSI, its insurer or its attorneys should reap an unearned windfall because of their success on the anti-SLAPP motion. If DSI, its insurer or its attorneys believe that the attorneys' hourly rates are below market, they could have negotiated for what they believe are market rates. Per the standard of what generally constitutes a market rate - what an attorney and client freely negotiate without external constraints - the billed hourly rates are the market rates. The hours claimed by DSI's attorneys are reduced by the time identified in Music Group's initial opposition memorandum for work spent on matters other than the anti-SLAPP motion. The resulting number of hours is reduced by a negative multiplier of .1 to express the court's considerable distaste for the extraordinarily excessive request made by DSI in lieu of the court exercising its discretion to award no fees at all due the excessive request. Music Group has not shown that any of the costs sought by DSI are not recoverable. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Music Group is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

Defendant Anthony Karavidas' motion for attorneys' fees and costs is granted in part. The parties are directed to meet and confer and arrive at an amount of fees and costs that Mr. Karavidas is awarded against plaintiff Music Group Commercial BM Ltd. in conformity with this order. The billed hourly rates represent the market rate for the time spent by Mr. Karavidas' attorneys on his successful anti-SLAPP motion. There is no reason why Mr. Karavidas, his insurer or his attorneys should reap an unearned windfall because of their success on the anti-SLAPP motion. If Mr. Karavidas, his insurer or his attorneys believe that the attorneys' hourly rates are below market, they could have negotiated for what they believe are market rates. Per the standard of what generally constitutes a market rate - what an attorney and client freely negotiate without external constraints - the billed hourly rates are the market rates. The hours claimed by Mr. Karavidas' attorneys are reduced by the time identified in Music Group's initial opposition memorandum for work spent on matters other than the anti-SLAPP motion. The resulting number of hours is reduced by a negative multiplier of .1 to express the court's considerable distaste for the extraordinarily excessive request made by Mr. Karavidas in lieu of the court exercising its discretion to award no fees at all due the excessive request. Music Group has not shown that any of the costs sought by Mr. Karavidas are not recoverable. rulings.Case Title: RICHARD FIERO VS. GOLDEN GATE BRIDGE, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Filing Fee For Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Subpoenas Of Plaintiffs Psychological Records)Declaration Of Tania Rose In Support Of Plntf Richard Fiero'S Motion To Quash Subpoenas Of Plntf'S Psychological/Psychiatric Medical Records
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 22, 2018, Line 6, PLAINTIFF RICHARD FIERO'S Motion To Quash Subpoenas Of Plaintiffs Psychological Records)Declaration Of Tania Rose In Support Of Plntf Richard Fiero'S Motion To Quash Subpoenas Of Plntf'S Psychological/Psychiatric Medical Records Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff Richard Fiero's Motion to Quash Deposition Subpoenas to Cio Hernandez, Nancy Rhine, and Scott Barshak, MD, is GRANTED and DENIED as follows. The Court, in its own discretion, will hear the late-filed Motion. Plaintiff has placed his mental state at issue in this case by alleging disability discrimination based upon preexisting depression. Furthermore, plaintiff has alleged that he "suffers and continues to suffer from¿emotional distress¿" (Compl., par. 43). However, "while the filing of a lawsuit may implicitly bring about a partial waiver of one's constitutional right of associational privacy, the scope of such 'waiver' must be narrowly rather than expansively construed, so that plaintiffs will not be unduly deterred from instituting lawsuits by the fear of exposure of their private associational affiliations and activities." Vinson v. Superior Court (1987) 43 Cal.3d 833, 842 quoting Britt v. Superior Court (1978) 20 Cal.3d 844, 859. Defendants must establish specific facts justifying inquiry into plaintiff's private life or show how such discovery would be relevant. Id. at 844. The subpoenas are limited to exclude billing records, pursuant to the agreement of the parties. The subpoenas are further limited to documents relating to depression (both pre and post termination) and symptoms of humiliation, emotional distress, and mental pain and anguish that post-date the termination. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcatalanotti@fmglaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DEBT RECOVERY RESOURCES, LLC. VS. DEMAKAS PLUMBING & HEATING, INC. A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 22, 2018, Line 7, DEFENDANT DEMAKAS PLUMBING & HEATING, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Demakas Plumbing & Heating, Inc.'s motion to strike the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Davis & Jones, LLC is denied. The first amended complaint is filed under the name of the purported legal name of Debt Recovery Resources. A business entity is required to use its legal name, not a DBA or trade name, and that is what Davis & Jones claims to have done here. Even if Davis & Jones was required to seek leave to do so, which is at best uncertain, there is no reason to deny it leave had it done so and therefore no reason to grant this motion. This is particularly true since Demakas has failed to identify any legal prejudice to it by permitting the case to go forward with Davis & Jones as the plaintiff. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Davis & Jones is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: SHU XU VS. SCUDERIA WEST ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE GENERAL DENIAL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 22, 2018, Line 10, PLAINTIFF SHU XU'S MOTION TO STRIKE GENERAL DENIAL. Plaintiff Shu Xu's motion to strike affirmative defenses 4, 8, 9, 11, 12, 15, 16, 17, 19, 20, 21, 23, 24, 27, 29 and 34 in the answer filed by defendant NGCW, Inc. and The Western Surety Company to the first amended complaint is granted as to affirmative defense 34 and denied as to all other affirmative defenses. Affirmative defense 34 purporting to reserve a right to add additional affirmative defenses without leave of court is invalid. It is premature to determine with certitude whether plaintiff is correct that some or all of the affirmative defenses are inapplicable to any of the claims asserted by the plaintiff. At this early stage of the case it is not possible to determine whether defendants will be able to develop any factual support for any of these affirmative defenses. While defendants have not alleged any facts in support of their affirmative defenses, such conclusory pleading of affirmative defenses comports with longstanding practice in Bay Area state courts. The appropriate course for a plaintiff when faced with an array of affirmative defenses many of which appear to be unsupported and/or insupportable is not to bring a pleading motion, but instead to serve discovery such as form interrogatory 15.0 and, after the defendant has had adequate time to conduct factual investigation and take discovery of its own, confer with the defendant to determine which, if any of the affirmative defenses defendant intends to pursue and, if there is a dispute at that time, plaintiff can bring a motion for judgment on pleadings or a motion for summary adjudication directed to the affirmative defenses that are truly in dispute. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for NGCW and Western Surety is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: SHU XU VS. SCUDERIA WEST ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO GENERAL DENIAL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 22, 2018, Line 10, PLAINTIFF SHU XU'S DEMURRER TO GENERAL DENIAL. Plaintiff Shu Xu's demurrer to all 34 affirmative defenses in the answer filed by defendant NGCW, Inc. and The Western Surety Company is sustained without leave to amend as to affirmative defense 34 and overruled as to all other affirmative defenses. Affirmative defense 34 purporting to reserve a right to add additional affirmative defenses without leave of court is invalid. It is premature to determine with certitude whether plaintiff is correct that some or all of the affirmative defenses are inapplicable to any of the claims asserted by the plaintiff. At this early stage of the case it is not possible to determine whether defendants will be able to develop any factual support for any of these affirmative defenses. While defendants have not alleged any facts in support of their affirmative defenses, such conclusory pleading of affirmative defenses comports with longstanding practice in Bay Area state courts. The appropriate course for a plaintiff when faced with an array of affirmative defenses many of which appear to be unsupported and/or insupportable is not to bring a pleading motion, but instead to serve discovery such as form interrogatory 15.0 and, after the defendant has had adequate time to conduct factual investigation and take discovery of its own, confer with the defendant to determine which, if any of the affirmative defenses defendant intends to pursue and, if there is a dispute at that time, plaintiff can bring a motion for judgment on pleadings or a motion for summary adjudication directed to the affirmative defenses that are truly in dispute. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for NGCW and Western Surety is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: DWIGHT A. LUM ET AL VS. ZEPPELIN W. WONG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Dwight A Lums Responses To Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 22, 2018, Line 5, DEFENDANT VELDA WONG'S Motion To Compel Dwight A Lums Responses To Interrogatories Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is GRANTED as follows: Special Interrogatories: 7-14, 26-34, 46-54, 60-69, 88-89, 93-100; Form Interrogatories: 15.1, 17.1, 50.1-.2. Verified amended code-compliant responses are due within two weeks of notice of this order. Sanctions in the amount of $2500 are payable against plaintiffs and their counsel of records. Sanctions are due within two weeks of notice of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcatalanotti@fmglaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested =(302/JPT)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Venue
